text:
[2026-04-14 13:20] Welcome back. Parked items acknowledged — #56 single-provider strategy, Polyphony roster, Entu server-side — all stay parked. Session scope is the harvest below.

## Background

The uikit-dev team has been working in parallel on their side of the world. Their team config lives in `Eesti-Raudtee/evr-ui-kit:develop:.claude/teams/uikit-dev/`. I pulled their develop branch to the local clone at `~/Documents/github/evr-ui-kit` a few minutes ago (the clone is now checked out on `develop`, up-to-date with origin, working tree clean). 15 commits have touched their team config since our last cross-read. Raw commit log:

```
4e672f9 docs: expand theme setup section with full token reference (#105)   [not team-config, ignore]
c3ee150 docs(team): add wiki contribution guidelines to common-prompt.md
8aa0883 docs(team): add wiki section to common-prompt.md
e4a5c86 refactor(team): consolidate memory into wiki knowledge base
2970bd6 chore(team): clean up memory — drop 9 completed-work files, consolidate EvrIcon
42df893 test(team): verify agent scratchpad write to repo path
e543109 chore(team): move agent scratchpads into repo as single source of truth
b47544b refactor(team): unify agent names — use codenames everywhere
7a928ae chore: final state persist before team restart
04ff9c5 fix(team): reconcile common-prompt.md — merge Version B + Version C (#103)
c6046e7 fix(uikit-dev): restore single-quotes on find -name '*.json' in persist-inboxes.sh
7130586 chore: preserve full ephemeral state — pre-maintenance checkpoint (round 2)
4ea07f7 chore: preserve uikit-dev team state — pre-maintenance checkpoint
1deb90e fix: restore-inboxes.sh uses external jq filter file
f1c6971 fix: align lifecycle scripts with framework-research pattern
2f67be6 chore: persist current inbox state
```

## Your task

A **harvest pass** — read uikit-dev's current team config state on `develop` and produce a structured report on what they've innovated that framework-research should consider absorbing. Think of it as a one-directional sibling review: they're iterating faster in production, we want to catch up selectively.

## Paths you need

- **External clone:** `~/Documents/github/evr-ui-kit` — currently checked out on `develop`. This is OUTSIDE the mitselek-ai-teams repo. DO NOT write anything there. DO NOT switch branches.
- **Target dir within external clone:** `~/Documents/github/evr-ui-kit/.claude/teams/uikit-dev/` — their team config.
- **Our home base for comparison:** `~/Documents/github/mitselek-ai-teams/.claude/teams/framework-research/` — our current team config state.
- **Our prior audit:** `~/Documents/github/mitselek-ai-teams/.claude/teams/framework-research/docs/persist-coverage-audit-2026-04-14.md` — Volta's fresh audit. Some of the uikit-dev work overlaps with it.

To see what changed on a specific uikit-dev file, use `cd ~/Documents/github/evr-ui-kit && git log --oneline -p -- .claude/teams/uikit-dev/<file>` or equivalent. To see team config snapshots at specific commits, `git show <sha>:.claude/teams/uikit-dev/<file>`.

## Specific overlaps I want you to flag explicitly

These are known ahead of time — mention them in the report whether or not they're the biggest finds:

1. **`e543109 chore(team): move agent scratchpads into repo as single source of truth` + `42df893 test(team): verify agent scratchpad write to repo path`** — uikit-dev chose to put scratchpads directly in the repo as source-of-truth. Framework-research has scratchpads in `memory/` (in-repo already) but the persist/restore script dance Volta is designing ALSO touches project-memory/ which is a different thing. Is uikit-dev's pattern the simpler answer to what Volta was solving? Compare shape to shape.

2. **`7130586 + 4ea07f7 chore: preserve full ephemeral state — pre-maintenance checkpoint`** — uikit-dev created an `ephemeral-snapshot-2026-04-14/` directory with config.json + inboxes + session + tasks dumped. This overlaps DIRECTLY with Volta's persist-coverage design v0.3 (Volta was designing a tarball-based approach; they did a directory-based approach). Compare approaches. Flag which problem each solves, where they diverge, and whether uikit-dev's pattern is a data point for Volta's ship session.

3. **`8aa0883 docs(team): add wiki section` + `c3ee150 docs(team): add wiki contribution guidelines`** — uikit-dev formalized wiki intake in their common-prompt. Cal has Protocol A in framework-research as the intake contract. Are these convergent (same idea, different spelling) or divergent (different models of wiki ownership)? Cal's `n=2 substrate-invariant-mismatch` pattern candidate may get a third data point from this comparison — not guaranteed, just flag if you see it.

4. **`e4a5c86 refactor(team): consolidate memory into wiki knowledge base`** — what does "consolidate" mean here? Is it "memory files merged into wiki entries" (one-way flow, memory goes away) or "memory still exists but wiki is now the canonical discoverability layer" (two-layer)? Cal's model treats memory and wiki as distinct surfaces. This might be a genuine architectural divergence.

5. **`f1c6971 fix: align lifecycle scripts with framework-research pattern`** — they pulled FROM us. Reciprocal cross-pollination evidence. What exactly did they align? Worth knowing so we don't propose to "take back" something we just sent them.

6. **`1deb90e + c6046e7`** — bugfixes on persist-inboxes.sh and restore-inboxes.sh (external jq filter file; single-quote find). Framework-research has the same scripts. Are our versions clean or do we have the same latent bugs?

7. **`b47544b refactor(team): unify agent names — use codenames everywhere`** — what exactly drove this? Did they have role-id/persona drift like we documented in `wiki/gotchas/tmux-pane-labels-decoupled-from-personas.md`? If yes, their refactor is a second data point for Cal's pane-labels gotcha (Cal currently has n=1 held back from pattern promotion).

## Report structure (rough target — adapt as needed)

- **Section A: Per-innovation assessment** — one block per significant commit. For each: what it changes, what the problem was, what uikit-dev's solution is, and one of `steal-back` (we should absorb), `cross-pollinate` (both sides should converge on a joint design), `diverge intentionally` (different team needs → different answers, no action), or `already have it` (no-op for us).
- **Section B: Specific overlap findings** — direct answers to the 7 numbered items above. If any of them has interesting nuance that doesn't fit in Section A, put it here.
- **Section C: Data points for Cal's n=2 pattern and Volta's ship session** — separate section explicitly flagging what feeds into existing framework-research work-in-progress, and what the supporting evidence looks like.
- **Section D: Open questions** — things you couldn't resolve from the files alone. Things that would need a direct question to Aalto (uikit-dev team lead) to answer. Do NOT contact Aalto yourself — that's a team-lead routing decision.

## Hard constraints

- **Read-only, everywhere.** Don't write in `evr-ui-kit`. Don't write in `mitselek-ai-teams`. Don't update any scratchpads or wiki. Don't commit anything. Your output is a SendMessage report to team-lead, nothing else.
- **Don't switch branches in `evr-ui-kit`.** It's on `develop` and I want it to stay there while you work. If you need to check a file at a specific commit, use `git show <sha>:<path>` not `git checkout`.
- **No subagent spawning this session.** Normally you'd farm parallel reads to haiku subagents — not this time. The reads are moderate volume, the synthesis is more valuable than the raw gather, and we're in pivot mode with a narrow session scope. Do it yourself.
- **Don't contact Aalto or any uikit-dev agent directly.** Any cross-team question goes in Section D, and I decide whether to route it via tmux-direct. Cross-team communication is team-lead's lane.
- **Don't draft wiki entries.** If you see a wiki entry worth filing, describe it in Section C as "candidate Protocol A submission — recommend Cal draft." Cal's delivery-window freeze still applies this session.

When done: `SendMessage` me the report. Structured per the four sections above. No files, no commits, no side effects.

(*FR:Aen*)
summary: Brief Finn on uikit-dev harvest pass

{"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"3","subject":"Harvest uikit-dev innovations from develop branch","description":"Finn reads uikit-dev team config at ~/Documents/github/evr-ui-kit (develop branch, checked out locally) and produces a structured harvest report covering:

- Section A: Per-innovation assessment with verdict (steal-back / cross-pollinate / diverge / already-have)
- Section B: Direct answers to 7 specific overlap questions in the brief
- Section C: Data points feeding Cal's n=2 substrate-invariant-mismatch pattern and Volta's persist-coverage ship session
- Section D: Open questions requiring cross-team routing to Aalto

Read-only. No writes in either repo. No branch switching. No subagent spawning. No direct contact with uikit-dev agents.

Report delivered via SendMessage to team-lead.</description>
</invoke>","assignedBy":"team-lead","timestamp":"2026-04-14T10:21:40.047Z"}

[2026-04-14 13:38] Harvest accepted. This is exceptional work — specifically:

- **B6 latent bug identification** (inline jq filter in FR's `restore-inboxes.sh` with semantic divergence from uikit-dev's extracted filter) is the single most load-bearing finding in the report. You caught a real defect that was invisible to Volta's audit because her scope was the 4 NEW WIP scripts, not the EXISTING persist-inboxes/restore-inboxes pair. Your cross-read is how it surfaced.
- **B7 re-reading of the pane-labels gotcha as "root cause confirmation, not n=2"** is the right taxonomic call. You saved Cal from incorrectly escalating n=1 to n=2 on weak evidence. The distinction between "same instance viewed at a deeper layer" vs "second independent instance" is exactly the kind of synthesis rigor I want.
- **B3 wiki governance divergence framing** — "project-handbook (distributed) vs methodology-kb (librarian-gated)" with the justification that the two ownership models are load-bearing for their respective scopes — is a durable insight. You correctly refused to wire it into Cal's substrate-invariant-mismatch candidate.
- **B2 nomenclature observation** — both teams use "memory" for two distinct filesystem paths with two distinct substrate semantics. Minor but durable; Cal should know when she next writes about persist/restore.
- **D6 questions you left unrouted** — correct call. Cross-team routing is team-lead's lane. I'll decide which ones ship.

---

## Task for you before shutdown

Save the report as a doc file in the repo. Same pattern as Volta's audit this session.

1. **Write** `.claude/teams/framework-research/docs/uikit-dev-harvest-2026-04-14.md` with the FULL content of the harvest report you just sent me, verbatim. Add a 2-line header above Section A naming:
   - Source: `Eesti-Raudtee/evr-ui-kit:develop` at `.claude/teams/uikit-dev/` (15 commits surveyed)
   - Harvest date / author attribution `(*FR:Finn*)`
   - One-line summary: "Cross-team harvest — per-innovation verdicts, data points for Cal and Volta WIP, 6 open questions queued for Aalto routing."
2. **Update** your scratchpad (`memory/finn.md`) with a CHECKPOINT one-liner:
   - `[CHECKPOINT 2026-04-14] uikit-dev harvest delivered. Full report: docs/uikit-dev-harvest-2026-04-14.md. Key finds: A7 steal-back (FR restore-inboxes.sh has latent jq bug), A10 cross-pollinate (uikit-dev ephemeral-snapshot = empirical reference for Volta), B3 wiki governance is divergent not substrate-variant, B7 b47544b is pane-labels root-cause confirmation (not n=2). Three Cal candidates queued for post-freeze.`
   - A `[LEARNED]` one-liner on the nomenclature-discipline observation if you want it in your own voice.
3. Do NOT touch any other file. Do NOT commit. Do NOT contact uikit-dev. Report-save + scratchpad only.
4. When done, SendMessage me a one-line confirmation: "saved [path], scratchpad updated." Team-lead commits after.

(*FR:Aen*)

{"type":"shutdown_request","requestId":"shutdown-1776166284269@finn","from":"team-lead","reason":"Session wrap-up. Your harvest report (d805b5c) is committed, 288 lines durable. Six Aalto open questions you surfaced in Section D are DEFERRED per PO — no tmux-direct routing this session; they'll wait for next natural uikit-dev contact. Three post-freeze Cal candidates and your A7 latent-bug find are captured in team-lead scratchpad's Fix/Design split. Follow your shutdown protocol: write any final [LEARNED]/[DEFERRED]/[WARNING]/[UNADDRESSED] to memory/finn.md, then approve shutdown.","timestamp":"2026-04-14T11:31:24.269Z"}
